title: 'Hamnet' Was the Biggest Winner of the 2026 Golden Globes Weekend
text: Director Chloé Zhao's film emerged as the standout victor during the awards weekend, despite Paul Thomas Anderson's One Battle After Another securing more individual Golden Globe awards. Hamnet clinched Best Motion Picture – Drama at the 83rd Golden Globes ceremony, held January 11, 2026 at The Beverly Hilton. The victory concluded the main awards show and positioned the film as a formidable Oscar contender heading into nomination voting. The film's dominance extended beyond the Golden Globes themselves. It previously won Best Picture at the AARP Movies for Grownups Awards, with presenter Sharon Stone expressing emotion about how the film helps audiences wake you up to the understanding of what you have. Leading into the Golden Globes weekend, Hamnet accumulated significant industry recognition, including People's Choice Award at TIFF and nominations from the PGA, DGA, and SAG Awards. The film had received placement on 14 BAFTA longlists, suggesting strong international recognition. While One Battle After Another remains the presumed frontrunner for the Academy Awards, the article notes historical precedent: the last two films winning Best Director, Best Screenplay, and a Best Picture award at the Golden Globes were La La Land and The Social Network, suggesting Hamnet could potentially achieve an upset victory.
